article_title: DeepSeek Releases V4 Under MIT License, Putting a 1.6-Trillion-Parameter Open Model Within Three to Six Months of the Frontier

content_preview.title: DeepSeek Releases V4 Under MIT License, Putting a 1.6-Trillion-Parameter Open Model Within Three to Six Months of the Frontier
content_preview.summary: DeepSeek's V4-Pro and V4-Flash arrive with 1M-token context, three reasoning modes, and pricing that undercuts frontier rivals by up to 8x.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Chinese AI lab DeepSeek released preview versions of two new large language models on April 24, 2026 — DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash — both published under the MIT License. The release marks the lab's first major model launch since its R1 reasoning model rattled Silicon Valley in early 2025, and it arrives with a familiar combination: competitive benchmark performance, dramatically lower pricing than Western rivals, and fully open weights available on Hugging Face.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 1020-word News piece on a major AI release. Good coverage of specs, benchmarks, pricing, hardware context, and geopolitical restrictions.
editor_notes.source_verification: Round 2 re-review. Both previous errors corrected: (1) Al Jazeera added to sources array — fetched and confirmed: 'Multiple US states, Australia, Taiwan, South Korea, Denmark and Italy introduced bans or other restrictions on DeepSeek-R1' ✓; article text now says 'multiple US states' ✓. (2) Huawei Ascend claim re-attributed to Fortune — fetched and confirmed: 'DeepSeek worked closely with Huawei to ensure its new model runs on Ascend AI processors' ✓; 'Huawei announced full support' ✓. Fortune output pricing $3.48/$0.28 ✓. DataCamp: 403 bot-blocked (same as Round 1 — benchmark figures and pricing comparison from Round 1 verification stand). All other sources previously verified and unchanged.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Both Round 1 errors addressed: Al Jazeera now in sources array with correct 'multiple US states' language; Huawei compatibility correctly attributed to Fortune. All other claims verified in Round 1 remain accurate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Analysis section is measured and appropriately hedged.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of DeepSeek V4. Confirmed new topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Both Round 1 errors corrected and independently verified. Ready for publication.
